channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: OpenAI Codex Security Scanned 1.2 Million Commits and Found 10,561 High-Severity Issues
item.description: OpenAI on Friday began rolling out Codex Security, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered security agent that's designed to find, validate, and propose fixes for vulnerabilities. The feature is available in a research preview to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, Business, and Edu customers via the Codex web with free usage for the next month. "It builds deep context about your project to identify
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic Finds 22 Firefox Vulnerabilities Using Claude Opus 4.6 AI Model
item.description: Anthropic on Friday said it discovered 22 new security vulnerabilities in the Firefox web browser as part of a security partnership with Mozilla. Of these, 14 have been classified as high, seven have been classified as moderate, and one has been rated low in severity. The issues were addressed in Firefox 148, released late last month. The vulnerabilities were identified over a two-week period in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI Investigates Suspicious Activity in Surveillance Platform
item.description: <p>The FBI is investigating suspicious cyber activity in a system used to process surveillance and wiretap warrants, raising concerns about security risks to sensitive data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fbi-investigates-cyber-activity-surveillance-warrant-system/">FBI Investigates Suspicious Activity in Surveillance Platform</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid in Byzantine Monk Cooking
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: North Korean APTs Use AI to Enhance IT Worker Scams
item.description: DPRK worker scams are old hat, but they're still working, thanks to AI tools that help with everything from face swapping to daily emails.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: <p><a href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/27/technology/openai-agreement-pentagon-ai.html">OpenAI is in</a> and <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/28/openai-us-military-anthropic">Anthropic is out</a> as a supplier of AI technology for the US defense department. This news caps a week of bluster by the highest officials in the US government towards some of the wealthiest titans of the big tech industry, and the overhanging specter of the existential risks posed by a new technology powerful enough that the Pentagon claims it is essential to national security. At issue is Anthropic&#8217;s <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/news/statement-department-of-war">insistence</a> that the US Department of Defense (DoD) could not use its models to facilitate &#8220;mass surveillance&#8221; or &#8220;fully autonomous weapons,&#8221; provisions the defense secretary Pete Hegseth ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: LexisNexis Hack Exposes 3.9M Records Through Unpatched React Vulnerability
item.description: <p>LexisNexis confirmed a data breach after hackers leaked stolen files, with attackers claiming they exploited the React2Shell vulnerability.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-lexisnexis-breach-3-9m-records-react-vulnerability/">LexisNexis Hack Exposes 3.9M Records Through Unpatched React Vulnerability</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Iran's MuddyWater Hackers Hit US Firms with New 'Dindoor' Backdoor
item.description: A bank, an airport, a non-profit and the Israeli branch of a US software company were among the targets of this new MuddyWater campaign

item.title: Transparent Tribe Uses AI to Mass-Produce Malware Implants in Campaign Targeting India
item.description: The Pakistan-aligned threat actor known as Transparent Tribe has become the latest hacking group to embrace artificial intelligence (AI)-powered coding tools to strike targets with various implants. The activity is designed to produce a "high-volume, mediocre mass of implants" that are developed using lesser-known programming languages like Nim, Zig, and Crystal and rely on trusted services like
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: EU Auto Rules Shift Gears on Cybersecurity Standards
item.description: The European Union is taking new precautions as climate change and cybersecurity threats rise across the automotive industry.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 2–6.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-breaches-and-industry-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Bitwarden Brings Passkey Logins to Windows 11, Expanding Passwordless Sign-Ins
item.description: <p>Bitwarden now supports passkey logins on Windows 11 for Microsoft Entra ID users, extending passwordless sign-ins to device authentication.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-bitwarden-passkey-windows-11-sign-in/">Bitwarden Brings Passkey Logins to Windows 11, Expanding Passwordless Sign-Ins</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Multi-Stage VOID#GEIST Malware Delivering XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a multi-stage malware campaign that uses batch scripts as a pathway to deliver various encrypted remote access trojan (RATs) payloads that correspond to XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT. The stealthy attack chain has been codenamed VOID#GEIST by Securonix Threat Research. At a high level, the obfuscated batch script is used to deploy a second
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran's Cyber-Kinetic War Doctrine Takes Shape
item.description: Iran has been hacking IP cameras to plan missile strikes against its enemies, and mounting other attacks on physical assets, showing how cyber and kinetic warfare are fast becoming one in the same.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Cyberattack on Mexico's Gov't Agencies Highlight AI Threat
item.description: Using Anthropic's Claude, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and a detailed playbook prompt, a handful of cyberattackers reportedly gained access to government agencies and its citizens' data.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Zero‑Day Attacks on Enterprise Software Reach Record High, Google Warns
item.description: Almost a quarter of the zero days detected by Google in 2025 targeted security and networking appliances

item.title: Claude Used to Hack Mexican Government
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that model. When done right, it builds client trust, increases upsell opportunities, and drives recurring revenue. But to deliver this consistently and efficiently, you need the right technology and processes.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Hackers Target U.S. Networks With New Dindoor Backdoor
item.description: New research from Broadcom's Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter Team has discovered evidence of an Iranian hacking group embedding itself in several U.S. companies' networks, including banks, airports, non-profit, and the Israeli arm of a software company. The activity has been attributed to a state-sponsored hacking group called MuddyWater (aka Seedworm). It's affiliated with the Iranian
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What cybersecurity actually does for your business
item.description: The ability to continue operating safely in an unsafe environment where competitors cannot is a competitive advantage that is rarely measured or discussed

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Use TernDoor, PeerTime, BruteEntry in South American Telecom Attacks
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been targeting critical telecommunications infrastructure in South America since 2024, targeting Windows and Linux systems and edge devices with three different implants. The activity is being tracked by Cisco Talos under the moniker UAT-9244, describing it as closely associated with another cluster known as FamousSparrow. It's worth
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Reveals ClickFix Campaign Using Windows Terminal to Deploy Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft on Thursday disclosed details of a new widespread ClickFix social engineering campaign that has leveraged the Windows Terminal app as a way to activate a sophisticated attack chain and deploy the Lumma Stealer malware. The activity, observed in February 2026, makes use of the terminal emulator program instead of instructing users to launch the Windows Run dialog and paste a command
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Hikvision and Rockwell Automation products to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The critical-severity vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2017-7921 (CVSS score: 9.8) - An improper authentication vulnerability affecting
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
item.description: Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Hackers Used New Exploit Kit to Compromise Thousands of iPhones
item.description: <p>Thousands of iPhones were compromised using the Coruna exploit kit, which chained 23 iOS vulnerabilities into advanced attacks used for espionage and cybercrime.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-coruna-exploit-kit-thousands-of-iphones-compromised/">Hackers Used New Exploit Kit to Compromise Thousands of iPhones</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Cisco Drops 48 New Firewall Vulnerabilities, 2 Critical
item.description: Edge bugs are so fetch, and Cisco just patched 50 new ones, including some heavy hitters with 10 out of 10 scores on the CVSS scale.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: How to Comply with HECVAT in 2026 (Free Checklist) | UpGuard
item.description: If you're a vendor supporting the education sector, this checklist will help you prepare for any HECVAT questionnaires that come your way.

item.title: Israel Hacked Traffic Cameras in Iran
item.description: <p><a href="https://www.timesofisrael.com/report-israel-hacked-tehran-traffic-cameras-to-track-khamenei-ahead-of-assassination/">Multiple</a> <a href="https://www.ft.com/content/bf998c69-ab46-4fa3-aae4-8f18f7387836">news</a> <a href="https://www.channelnewsasia.com/world/iran-war-inside-plan-kill-ali-khamenei-5966861">outlets</a> are reporting on Israel&#8217;s hacking of Iranian traffic cameras and how they assisted with the killing of that country&#8217;s leadership.</p> <p><i>The New York Times</i> has an &#60;a href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/01/us/politics/cia-israel-ayatollah-compound.html"&#60;article</a> on the intelligence operation more generally.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Software Development Practices Help Enterprises Tackle Real-Life Risks
item.description: Organizations can borrow secure-by-design processes to manage non-technical challenges like governance or the inevitable human error.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: AI-Driven Insider Risk Now a “Critical Business Threat,” Report Warns
item.description: Malicious insiders are using misusing AI for nefarious gain, while employees cutting corners also creates risk, warns Mimecast

item.title: Cisco Confirms Active Exploitation of Two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Cisco has disclosed that two more vulnerabilities affecting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly SD-WAN vManage) have come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below -
CVE-2026-20122 (CVSS score: 7.1) - An arbitrary file overwrite vulnerability that could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to overwrite arbitrary files on the local file system.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Preparing for the Quantum Era: Post-Quantum Cryptography Webinar for Security Leaders
item.description: Most organizations assume encrypted data is safe. But many attackers are already preparing for a future where today’s encryption can be broken. Instead of trying to decrypt information now, they are collecting encrypted data and storing it so it can be decrypted later using quantum computers. This tactic—known as “harvest now, decrypt later”—means sensitive data transmitted today could become
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
item.description: Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: ContextCrush Flaw Exposes AI Development Tools to Attacks
item.description: Critical flaw "ContextCrush" in Context7 MCP Server could allow malicious instructions into AI tools

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: DDR5 Bot Scalping, Samsung TV Tracking, Reddit Privacy Fine & More
item.description: Some weeks in cybersecurity feel routine. This one doesn&rsquo;t. Several new developments surfaced over the past few days, showing how quickly the threat landscape keeps shifting. Researchers uncovered fresh activity, security teams shared new findings, and a few unexpected moves from major tech companies also drew attention. Together, these updates offer a useful snapshot of what is happening
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Coruna Exploit Kit Targets Older iPhones in Multi-Stage Campaigns
item.description: Exploit kit "Coruna" targets iPhones running iOS 13.0 to 17.2.1, focusing on financial data theft

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of never-before-seen malware. Zscaler ThreatLabz, which observed the activity in January 2026, is tracking the cluster under the name Dust Specter. The attacks, which manifest in the form of two different
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacked App Part of US/Israeli Propaganda Campaign Against Iran
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong. Attackers still compromise networks every day using valid credentials. The issue is not MFA itself, but coverage.&nbsp; Enforced through an identity provider (IdP) such as Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, or
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Zero-Click FreeScout Bug Enables Remote Code Execution
item.description: Ox Security warns that Mail2Shell could enable threat actors to hijack FreeScout systems without user interaction

item.title: Cisco Issues Patches for 48 Vulnerabilities in Enterprise Networking Products
item.description: Two of the 48 Cisco vulnerabilities, affecting Secure Firewall Management Center, are maximum-severity flaws

item.title: APT28-Linked Campaign Deploys BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Russian cyber campaign that has targeted Ukrainian entities with two previously undocumented malware families named BadPaw and MeowMeow. "The attack chain initiates with a phishing email containing a link to a ZIP archive. Once extracted, an initial HTA file displays a lure document written in Ukrainian concerning border crossing appeals
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How SMBs use threat research and MDR to build a defensive edge
item.description: We speak to Director of ESET Threat Research Jean-Ian Boutin about where solutions that blend advanced technology with human expertise provide the most practical value for businesses

item.title: Europol Operation Seizes LeakBase Data Breach Site
item.description: A global operation has resulted in the takedown of popular cybercrime forum LeakBase

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Takes Down Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Linked to 64,000 Attacks
item.description: Tycoon 2FA, one of the prominent phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) toolkits that allowed cybercriminals to stage adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) credential harvesting attacks at scale, was dismantled by a coalition of law enforcement agencies and security companies. The subscription-based phishing kit, which first emerged in August 2023, was described by Europol as one of the largest phishing
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI and Europol Seize LeakBase Forum Used to Trade Stolen Credentials
item.description: A joint law enforcement operation has dismantled LeakBase, one of the world's largest online forums for cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and cybercrime tools. The LeakBase forum, per the U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ), had over 142,000 members and more than 215,000 messages between members as of December 2025. Those attempting to access the forum's website ("leakbase[.]la") are now
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Vendor Tiering Series: Mapping Tiers to Inherent Risk | UpGuard
item.description: Subjective labels do not categorize vendor risk. Learn how to build a defensible tiering architecture using objective criteria and a scalable model.

item.title: VMware Aria Operations Bug Exploited, Cloud Resources at Risk
item.description: Exploitation of the command injection flaw in VMware Aria Operations could grant an attacker broad acess to victims' cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Perplexity AI Browser Flaw Could Let Calendar Invites Access Local Files
item.description: <p>Researchers say a vulnerability in Perplexity’s Comet AI browser could expose local files and credentials through malicious calendar invites.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-perplexity-comet-browser-vulnerability-local-files/">Perplexity AI Browser Flaw Could Let Calendar Invites Access Local Files</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hacktivist threat in the Middle East is highly lopsided, with two groups, Keymous+ and DieNet, driving nearly 70% of all attack activity between February 28 and March 2," Radware said in a Tuesday
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Stranger Things Meets Cybersecurity: Lessons from the Hive Mind
item.description: Events and concepts from the Stranger Things television series illustrate how enterprises can defend their networks and stay &quot;right side up.&quot;
item.dc:creator: Nadir Izrael

item.title: Coalition of Western Countries Launches 6G Cybersecurity Guidelines
item.description: A coalition of seven Western nations has launched guidelines to help integrate security-by-design principles into future 6G standards

item.title: Global Takedown Neutralizes Tycoon2FA Phishing Service
item.description: Law enforcers and industry partners have taken down notorious phishing-as-a-service platform Tycoon2FA

item.title: Google’s Biggest Android Security Update in Years Fixes 129 Bugs, Including an Actively Exploited Zero-Day
item.description: <p>Google’s March 2026 Android update patches 129 flaws, including an actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day, and urges users to install 2026-03-05.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-security-update-129-vulnerabilities/">Google’s Biggest Android Security Update in Years Fixes 129 Bugs, Including an Actively Exploited Zero-Day</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Dark Reading Confidential: This Threat Hunter Helped Cops Bust Up An African Cybercrime Syndicate
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 15: Interpol relied on Will Thomas and team to help break up a sprawling cybercrime ring, leading to the arrest of 574 suspects, the recovery of more than $3 million, and the decryption of six malware variants. Here's his story.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Surge in Attacks on Surveillance Cameras Linked to Iranian Hackers
item.description: Increased attempts to compromise surveillance cameras linked to Iran during Middle East conflict

item.title: Multi-Stage "BadPaw" Malware Campaign Targets Ukraine
item.description: Malware campaign uses Ukrainian email service for credibility, deploying "BadPaw" to execute attacks

item.title: Microsoft Helps Bust Global Hacking Service
Global coalition dismantles Tycoon 2FA phishing kit
Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five full iOS exploit chains and a total of 23 exploits, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said. It's not effective against the latest version of iOS. The findings were first reported by WIRED. "The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Manipulating AI Summarization Features
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroom: many organizations know they need "AI Governance," but they have no idea what they are actually looking for. The CISO’s Dilemma: You Have the AI Budget, but Do You Have the Requirements? As AI
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China's Silver Dragon Razes Governments in EU, SE Asia
item.description: The emerging actor, part of the APT41 nexus, gains initial access via phishing, and uses legitimate network services to obscure cyber espionage activities.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Calls for Global Digital Estate Standard as Posthumous Deepfake Fraud Risk Grows
item.description: The OpenID Foundation warns that fragmented policies on posthumous digital accounts could open the door for fraudsters to exploit AI deepfakes

item.title: Protecting education: How MDR can tip the balance in favor of schools
item.description: The education sector is notoriously short on cash, but rich in assets for threat actors to target. How can managed detection and response (MDR) help learning institutions regain the initiative?

item.title: Fake Laravel Packages on Packagist Deploy RAT on Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged malicious Packagist PHP packages masquerading as Laravel utilities that act as a conduit for a cross-platform remote access trojan (RAT) that's functional on Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. The names of the packages are listed below -
nhattuanbl/lara-helper (37 Downloads) nhattuanbl/simple-queue (29 Downloads) nhattuanbl/lara-swagger (49 Downloads)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: APT41-Linked Silver Dragon Targets Governments Using Cobalt Strike and Google Drive C2
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an advanced persistent threat (APT) group dubbed Silver Dragon that has been linked to cyber attacks targeting entities in Europe and Southeast Asia since at least mid-2024. "Silver Dragon gains its initial access by exploiting public-facing internet servers and by delivering phishing emails that contain malicious attachments," Check Point said
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations Flaw CVE-2026-22719 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting Broadcom VMware Aria Operations to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing active exploitation in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-22719 (CVSS score: 8.1), has been described as a case of command injection that could allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Indian APT 'Sloppy Lemming' Targets Defense, Critical Infrastructure
item.description: India-nexus cyber threat actors are growing more active and sophisticated, using custom tools coded in Rust and cloud-based command and control.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Vehicle Tire Pressure Sensors Enable Silent Tracking
item.description: Like many other features and systems in modern cars, tire pressure sensors leak sensitive data that can be abused by threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Qualcomm Zero-Day Exploited in Targeted Android Attacks
item.description: The exploitation activity against CVE-2026-21385, a high-severity memory corruption flaw, could be tied to commercial spyware or nation-state threat groups.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Announcing Risk Automations: From Discovery to Resolution In Seconds | UpGuard
item.description: Learn about UpGuard's new product launch. Transform your risk resolution engine by moving from alert to resolution in seconds, not days.

item.title: Speakeasies to Shadow AI: Banning AI Browsers Will Fail
item.description: Lessons from history highlight why AI-enabled browsers require controlled enablement.
item.dc:creator: Or Eshed

item.title: Chrome Extension Hijacked to Deliver Malware, Steal Crypto Wallets
item.description: <p>A compromised Chrome extension with 7,000 users was updated to deploy malware, strip security headers, and steal cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-compromised-chrome-extension-malware-crypto-theft/">Chrome Extension Hijacked to Deliver Malware, Steal Crypto Wallets</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data exfiltration or ransomware attack. The intrusions, identified by Huntress last month across five partner organizations, involved the threat actors using email spam as lures, followed by a phone call from
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacktivists Claim DHS Breach, Leak 6,600+ ICE Contractor Records
item.description: <p>Hacktivists claim they breached DHS systems, leaking records tied to 6,681 ICE contractor applicants, including major tech and defense firms.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dhs-ice-contractor-data-leak-hacktivist-claim/">Hacktivists Claim DHS Breach, Leak 6,600+ ICE Contractor Records</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Israel: RedAlert Spyware Campaign Exploits Wartime Panic With Trojanized App
item.description: Espionage campaign exploits Israel-Iran conflict, distributing a trojanized Red Alert app via SMS

item.title: AI and Deepfakes Supercharge Sophisticated Cyber-Attacks, Says Cloudflare
item.description: Cloudflare Threat Report warns that AI tools enable attackers who lacked required skills to generate effective attacks rapidly and at scale

item.title: Proofpoint Launches AI-Enabled Discovery & Archive Suite for Small to Medium Businesses
Building a High-Impact Tier 1: The 3 Steps CISOs Must Follow
item.description: Every CISO knows the uncomfortable truth about their Security Operations Center: the people most responsible for catching threats in real time are the people with the least experience. Tier 1 analysts sit at the front line of detection, and yet they are also the most vulnerable to the cognitive and organizational pressures that quietly erode SOC performance over time. The Paradox at the Gate:
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Leaked Database Sheds Light on Iranian Crypto Sanctions Evasion
item.description: Ariomex database reveals potential sanctions evasion and capital transfers tied to Iranian actors

item.title: Open-Source CyberStrikeAI Deployed in AI-Driven FortiGate Attacks Across 55 Countries
item.description: The threat actor behind the recently disclosed artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate appliances leveraged an open-source, AI-native security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI to execute the attacks. The new findings come from Team Cymru, which detected its use following an analysis of the IP address ("212.11.64[.]250") that was used by the suspected
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Half of US CISOs Work the Equivalent of a Six-Day Week
item.description: Seemplicity finds US security leaders work 11 or more extra hours per week

item.title: AI Agent Overload: How to Solve the Workload Identity Crisis
item.description: Workloads keep getting more complicated and organizations are struggling to keep up. So what's the play?
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: On Moltbook
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: As War Continues, Pro-Iranian Actors Launch Barrage of Cyberattacks
item.description: Iran and its supporters have taken to cyberspace to retaliate for US-Israeli military action, with an aim to cause economic and physical disruption.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
item.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. By providing structured access to applications, APIs, and data, MCP enables prompt-driven AI agents that can retrieve information, take action, and automate end-to-end business workflows across the enterprise. This is already showing up in production
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Starkiller Phishing Suite Uses AitM Reverse Proxy to Bypass Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new phishing suite called Starkiller that proxies legitimate login pages to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) protections. It's advertised as a cybercrime platform by a threat group calling itself Jinkusu, granting customers access to a dashboard that lets them select a brand to impersonate or enter a brand's real URL. It also lets
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Huge “Shadow Layer” of Organizations Hit by Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Black Kite reveals 26,000 unnamed corporate victims linked to 136 third-party breaches

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Actor Targets Iraqi Government Officials in AI-Powered Campaign
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz assessed with medium to high confidence that an Iranian adversary targeted Iraq’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a new cyber-attack

item.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
item.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activity, the company said, targets government and public-sector organizations with the end goal of redirecting victims to attacker-controlled infrastructure without stealing their tokens. It described
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-21385 (CVSS score: 7.8), a buffer over-read in the Graphics component. "Memory corruption when adding user-supplied data without checking available buffer space," Qualcomm said in an advisory,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SloppyLemming Targets Pakistan and Bangladesh Governments Using Dual Malware Chains
item.description: The threat activity cluster known as SloppyLemming has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting government entities and critical infrastructure operators in Pakistan and Bangladesh. The activity, per Arctic Wolf, took place between January 2025 and January 2026. It involves the use of two distinct attack chains to deliver malware families tracked as BurrowShell and a Rust-based
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description: The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: The Tug-of-War Over Firewall Backlogs in the AI-Driven Development Era
item.description: Speed and security are historically clashing priorities, but with AI and automation, it's increasingly important that application developers and security teams get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: 30 Alleged Members of 'The Com' Arrested in Project Compass
item.description: The global law enforcement crackdown, which began in January 2025, also identified nearly 180 members of the notorious cybercriminal collective.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Checkout.com
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0628 (CVSS score: 8.8), has been described as a case of insufficient policy enforcement in the WebView tag. It was patched by Google in early January 2026
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Develops Merkle Tree Certificates to Enable Quantum-Resistant HTTPS in Chrome
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
